title: 15 lakh Bengal voters in limbo due to missing e-signatures
content: EC officials said 60 lakh voters were initially marked for scrutiny after a special intensive revision (SIR) of rolls, with over 700 judicial officers assigned to review them. By March 23 evening, around 29 lakh cases had been cleared. Bengal CEO Manoj Agarwal said names carrying e-signatures till 5pm would be published in the first supplementary list. The list released around midnight carried about 10 lakh names.

title: ‘Put down the crown, clown’: Thousands rally against Trump in 'No Kings' protestss
content: Thousands of people joined “No Kings” protests across the United States and parts of Europe, opposing the war in Iran and the policies of Donald Trump. Minnesota became the focal point, with a major rally in St Paul led by Bruce Springsteen. While most protests were peaceful, some clashes were reported, and Republican leaders dismissed the demonstrations as politically motivated.

title: IPL 2026’s first controversy! Klaasen fumes over ‘debatable’ boundary catch
content: Royal Challengers Bengaluru secured a dominant win over Sunrisers Hyderabad, but a controversial catch by Phil Salt, which led to Heinrich Klaasen's dismissal, sparked debate. Despite the umpire's decision, replays suggested Salt might have touched the boundary sponge. Virat Kohli's unbeaten 69 then powered RCB's successful chase of 202.

title: PM Modi backs ethanol blending as key to cutting crude imports
content: “Farmers’ efforts in this have given a lot of relief to the country at this time of global crisis,” he said. Ethanol production, he added, has not only benefited the nation but also farmers, with approximately Rs 1.5 lakh crore worth of foreign exchange being saved. Jewar is a gateway to the sugarcane belts of western UP. Sugarcane feedstock is a key source of ethanol.

title: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang on how he manages 60 direct reportees who are mostly engineers
content: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang leads over sixty direct reports. He avoids one-on-one meetings, favoring collective problem-solving sessions. This approach ensures all team members receive the same information and can contribute. Huang believes in 'extreme co-design' and expects active participation. He is willing to address employees who do not contribute as expected.

title: Delhi shocker: Gang lures man using dating app, robs him of Rs 7 lakh
content: A man was lured to a meeting via a dating app in Nehru Place, only to be abducted, assaulted, and robbed of ₹7 lakh. Five individuals, including a woman, have been apprehended by Delhi Police following a thorough investigation involving CCTV analysis and digital tracking. The victim was forced to make UPI transfers before being abandoned.

title: IPL 2026: Virat Kohli scripts history, becomes the first player to…
content: Virat Kohli etched his name in IPL history, playing his 19th consecutive season for Royal Challengers Bengaluru.  He anchored RCB's chase with an unbeaten 69, guiding them to a six-wicket victory over Sunrisers Hyderabad.  Devdutt Padikkal's explosive 61 set the stage for the dominant win, while Ishan Kishan's 80 was the highlight for SRH.

title: ‘Dhurandhar: The Revenge’ makes over Rs 1,200 crore worldwide in 10 days
content: Ranveer Singh's 'Dhurandhar: The Revenge' is performing strongly at the box office, crossing Rs 1,200 crore worldwide after a 10-day run. The action-thriller saw a significant surge on its second Saturday, contributing to its impressive domestic and global collections. The film continues the story of an undercover agent battling terrorism.

title: Meta is rebranding employee titles, roles as it goes AI-native: What it said in memo
content: Meta's Reality Labs is undergoing a significant internal overhaul, rebranding employees as 'AI builders' and forming smaller, AI-focused teams. This strategic shift aims to boost engineering productivity and product quality, with CEO Mark Zuckerberg envisioning AI dramatically transforming work.  These new 'AI pods' will foster cross-functional collaboration, with dedicated leads managing operations and performance.

title: Bengaluru professor who called student 'terrorist' 13 times suspended
content: In a shocking turn of events, a professor from PES University has been suspended following the release of a video where he repeatedly referred to a minority student as a 'terrorist' during a lecture. The incident, which took place on March 24, has prompted the police to initiate a suo motu criminal case.

title: Iran reports strike at Bushehr nuclear plant as focus shifts to a new strait
content: An Iranian strike injured 12 US troops in Saudi Arabia, two of them seriously, in an attack on Prince Sultan airbase on Friday, two people briefed on the matter said. The combined missile and drone attack amounted to one of the most serious breaches of American air defences in the war. At least two KC-135 aerial refuelling planes also suffered significant damage.

title: CEOs of two of biggest American companies, Coca Cola & Walmart, are leaving due to AI
content: Two prominent CEOs, from Coca-Cola and Walmart, have cited the rise of AI as a factor in their decisions to step down. Both leaders indicated that the evolving business landscape, driven by AI, necessitates new leadership for future growth phases. Their departures highlight a significant technological shift impacting executive decision-making at major corporations.

title: Steyn gets Kohli's autograph on India jersey ahead of RCB vs SRH clash - Watch
content: Kohli then went on to register two milestones during the game between defending champions Royal Challengers Bengaluru and Sunrisers Hyderabad at the M Chinnaswamy Stadium. He became the first batter in IPL history to cross 4000 runs in run chases.

title: How an IIT-failed youtube teacher from Prayagraj ended up on the Forbes Billionaires list
content: Alakh Pandey, a teacher from Prayagraj, transformed his YouTube channel into the edtech giant PhysicsWallah, making quality education accessible and affordable for millions. His journey from free online lessons to a billion-dollar valuation highlights a unique path to success in the competitive Indian education market.

title: Man decapitates ice vendor, puts severed head on stove in Barabanki
content: In a disturbing turn of events in Barabanki, a middle-aged man allegedly decapitated an ice vendor following a heated dispute. After the brutal act, the perpetrator tried to incinerate the head inside his dwelling. Quick-thinking locals intervened, detaining him until the authorities arrived. A formal complaint has been filed against the man.

title: Fact check: Bradley Dack, Olivia Attwood cheating claims and marriage truth?
content: Bradley Dack is facing multiple cheating claims as several women speak out, according to The Sun. At the same time, Daily Mail reports reveal he and Olivia Attwood were never legally married despite their 2023 ceremony. The couple had planned to register later, but Olivia backed out after discovering issues, leading to their split earlier this year.

title: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce NDA speculation takes dramatic twist
content: Rumors about Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce enforcing strict NDAs at their wedding spread quickly after Graham Norton joked about signing them. Fans and media took it seriously, fueling speculation about a secretive ceremony. Norton later clarified it was a joke that got misreported. There is no confirmed NDA, and the couple has not shared any official wedding details yet.

title: Fact check: Did Cardi B really owe Stefon Diggs $1M?
content: Rumors linking Cardi B and Stefon Diggs went viral after a social media post claimed a fallout and a $1 million dispute. The speculation grew when an alleged deleted Instagram story surfaced. However, there is no verified proof supporting these claims. Neither party has responded publicly, and no credible reports confirm the narrative, making the controversy largely driven by unverified online speculation.

title: Expedite PNG clearances: Govt to municipal bodies, other agencies
content: Setting a target to add 50 lakh new piped gas connections to the existing base of 1.6 crore, at a round table here, the minister called for accelerating expansion on a mission mode, and underlined the need for single-window approvals, integration with urban planning, last-mile connectivity and convergence with ongoing urban initiatives.

title: IAF starts work on ‘Vayu Baan’, India’s first helicopter-dropped drone project
content: Vayu Baan is an indigenous air-launched effects (ALE) programme designed to integrate unmanned aerial systems directly with manned rotary-wing platforms. It is a small, autonomous drone designed to be dropped from a moving helicopter mid-flight. Once released, the drone unfolds its wings, activates its propulsion and operates as either a surveillance asset or a precision-guided munition (kamikaze drone).

title: Vulnerable need quick relief amid West Asia conflict: Finance ministry report
content: The report said the impact of the conflict on India will be felt through four channels: (a) supply disruptions to oil, gas and fertilisers and more importantly, to exports as well, (b) higher import prices, (c) higher logistics costs (e.g., freight and insurance) and (d) a possible decline in remittances by Indians in the Gulf countries. (The slowdown in exports to Gulf countries may not be particularly consequential for overall exports.)

title: PM, Saudi crown prince agree on need to keep shipping lines open
content: Underscoring India’s energy and diaspora interests in the Gulf, Modi has twice spoken to leaders of all GCC countries since the conflict started and condemned the violation of their sovereignty and territorial integrity. India, however, is yet to condemn the US-Israel strikes on Iran. This was the second telephonic conversation between the PM and the crown prince since the West Asia conflict started on Feb 28.

title: 80% Indians cleared by tests are struck by heart attack
content: At the core of the problem is a mismatch. Most of these risk calculators were designed using Western populations, where heart disease tends to occur later. In India, it strikes earlier and behaves differently — the study found the average age of patients was just 54, underscoring how premature heart disease has become.

title: NFL Trade Rumors: Colts eye franchise-altering $160M trade
content: The Indianapolis Colts are exploring options to fix their struggling pass rush after missing out on Trey Hendrickson. Trade buzz around Myles Garrett has linked him to Indianapolis, especially with a potential rule change easing future draft pick trades. With cap space and urgency, the Colts could pursue a bold move to land an elite defensive playmaker.

title: Vijaypat Singhania, ‘The Complete Man’, passes away
content: Vijaypat Singhania's family had acquired the company from ED Sassoon & Co in 1944, when it was primarily a manufacturer of woollen blankets. Under his leadership, Raymond evolved into a diversified conglomerate, expanding into synthetic fabrics, denim, steel, industrial files and cement, while also becoming one of India’s most recognised clothing brands.

title: India stands alone against China-backed WTO investment pact
content: While India is keen to find a solution, for almost 30 years, it has used this as a bargaining chip at every meeting, realising that an imposition of duty on streaming and downloads is set to generate revenue for it and others and put those such as the US at a disadvantage. But, for a change, India, the US, Russia and Pakistan are on the same side in their pitch for a reform of the 31-year-old multilateral body.

title: Why did Puka Nacua’s accuser suddenly back off? Legal twist fuels controversy
content: Puka Nacua received a legal boost after his accuser withdrew a temporary restraining order, though the civil lawsuit remains active. The Los Angeles Rams star denies all allegations. His attorney called the claims “meritless” and hinted at a defamation lawsuit, as the case continues to unfold alongside Nacua’s looming contract extension discussions.

title: Adani Defence delivers first batch of 2,000 LMGs to Army ahead of schedule
content: The delivery of the first batch of the 7.62 mm-calibre weapon, manufactured at the company’s small arms complex on the outskirts of Gwalior, was completed in seven months well ahead of the contracted schedule. The company spokesperson said the total order for the LMGs stands at around 40,000. The LMG weighs 8 kg and is 1,100 mm long and it has an effective range of 1,000 m, according to the company’s data. Prahar has a rate of fire of 600–750 rounds per minute.

title: Parliament to be adjourned on April 2, to meet after assembly polls
content: A fixed date for Parliament’s meeting to discuss what are likely to be two bills, one for delimitation based on 2011 census and another for amending constitutional provisions for the rollout of women’s quota in one-third of Lok Sabha and assembly seats, will convey the govt’s determination in no uncertain terms.

title: India denies Musk joined Modi-Trump call over war, refutes US media report
content: Quoting two anonymous US officials, the report said it was unclear why Musk was on the call or whether he spoke. “His companies have taken on significant investment from sovereign wealth funds in countries in the Middle East, including Saudi Arabia and Qatar. Musk has also long coveted a greater commercial presence in India,” it said.

title: Relief for drivers: No jail for 1st-time pollution, road safety norms violations
content: In the case of noise pollution-related violations, the bill proposes a warning for the first offence, while repeat offence will attract a penalty of up to Rs 10,000. Similarly, failure to comply with an order or contravening provisions of the Electricity Act or rules, which can currently land a person in jail for three months along with a fine of Rs 1 lakh, is proposed to be converted into a fine of Rs 10,000 to Rs 10 Lakh.

title: FSSAI makes food business permits permanent
content: Under new rules notified by Food Safety and Standards Authority of India (FSSAI), food licences and registrations will now remain valid permanently, unless cancelled for violations. This means lakhs of restaurants, small eateries and vendors will no longer need to renew their licences every few years. The move is part of a wider overhaul aimed at making compliance easier while tightening actual food safety checks.

title: After Zoji La avalanche deaths, Ladakh hill council stakes claim on Zero Point
content: Addressing a press conference in Drass, LAHDC-Kargil councillors praised the Army, civil administration and Ladakh Police for prompt rescue efforts and retrieval of bodies from the snow following the accident on Friday afternoon. However, they expressed surprise over “J&K Police investigating the incident on Saturday morning”. While J&K claimed the area to be part of its territory, its administrative machinery was always slow to respond when any mishap occurred there, they alleged.

title: Over 3000 J&K govt schools have under 10 students: Minister
content: Of the 3,192 such schools,1494 are in Jammu division and 1,698 in Kashmir, National Conference’s (NC) Itoo said in a written reply to a question from BJP legislator RS Pathania. In Jammu, Kathua district has the highest number of such schools at 508. In Kashmir, Baramulla district tops the list of such schools with 396.

title: Bangladesh denies lifting IPL broadcast ban, says suspension still in place
content: In a statement issued on Saturday and reported by Bangladesh news outlet Daily Star, the Bangladesh broadcasting ministry said the previous government’s order is still effective. It added that it has sought the opinion of the ministry of youth and sports, with a letter sent on March 25, 2026, and any further decision will depend on that response.

title: Virat Kohli drops sitter vs SRH, Anushka Sharma's reaction goes viral - Watch
content: Virat Kohli's dropped catch in the IPL opener against Sunrisers Hyderabad was a moment of drama, but he later redeemed himself by dismissing the same batter. Ishan Kishan's explosive 80 powered SRH to 201/9, despite a strong bowling performance from RCB's Jacob Duffy. The match saw several crucial wickets fall, with Kishan's innings being a highlight.

title: Massive blaze at Kuwait airport after Iran drone strike, fuel tanks hit
content: A massive blaze at Kuwait International Airport, triggered by a suspected Iranian drone strike on fuel storage facilities, has been extinguished after nearly 58 hours. The attack caused significant damage to radar systems, with Kuwait blaming Iran and its proxies. This incident escalates regional tensions as Iran expands its aerial campaign.

title: Phil Salt steals the show with outrageous one-handed catch to remove Ishan Kishan - Watch
content: Phil Salt's spectacular one-handed catch ended Ishan Kishan's blistering 80-run knock, a pivotal moment in the IPL 2026 opener.  Despite Kishan's brilliance, which anchored Sunrisers Hyderabad's innings, Salt's fielding heroics, including another debated boundary catch, shifted momentum. Hyderabad ultimately posted 201/9, a total that felt short after Salt's impactful contributions.

title: Anushka cheers virat, bonds with Mandhana, Jemimah at RCB IPL 2026 opener
content: Anushka Sharma made a stylish return to the IPL 2026 stands at the M Chinnaswamy Stadium, cheering for husband Virat Kohli. Joined by WPL stars Smriti Mandhana and Shreyanka Patil, her presence delighted fans. Social media buzzed with her elegant appearance and fans' requests for her to wear an RCB jersey.

title: RCB honour victims with special tribute during IPL opener at Chinnaswamy
content: Cricket returned to Bengaluru's M Chinnaswamy Stadium with a revamped fan experience, though a poignant tribute marked the anniversary of a past tragedy. While many praised smoother entry and improved security, some faced minor inconveniences with new protocols. Over 2,000 personnel ensured crowd management, leading to less congestion. A minute's silence honored victims before play commenced.

title: Kerala Assembly Polls 2026: Inflation, a major talking point ahead of polls
content: The spike in food prices has emerged as a major concern for the public. A long-standing welfare architecture in Kerala has attempted to cushion the effects of inflation, especially for the vulnerable. However, a few experts have raised doubts about the effectiveness of the State government’s market intervention measures

title: Bengaluru Metro services disrupted on Purple Line for maintenance
content: The temporary halt disrupted early morning commuters travelling along the busy Purple Line corridor, as the maintenance announcement came only on Sunday morning when the work began, leaving passengers with no time to alter their plans or arrange alternative modes of transport.

title: Prevent allotment of reserved election symbols of one party to a member of another party, urges a PIL plea before Madras High Court
content: Litigant says, it has become a common practice among members of political parties to contest in reserved symbols of alliance partners

title: High up, and soaring: Chennai’s rental market
content: From core city neighbourhoods to fast-growing suburbs, Chennai’s rental market is witnessing a sharp upswing. Nearly 49% of the households in the city still depend on rental accommodation, data show. More often than not, tenants are forced to compromise on space and budget. This report presents  a list of emerging rental trends

title: CRPF officer who lost leg in line of duty battling pain, stagnant career
content: Ajay Malik, an Assistant Commandant with CRPF, lost his leg after he stepped on an IED in Jharkhand forest; retired CAPF personnel say officers face career stagnation though they work under grave conditions

title: Six leaders from Uttarakhand BJP join Congress as party steps up attack on Dhami-led government
content: Congress general secretary and Uttarakhand in-charge Kumari Selja says the public’s trust in the party is rising amid a growing dissatisfaction with the BJP government in the State

title: PM Modi speaks to Crown Prince of Saudi Arabia; discusses West Asia situation
content: In his telephonic conversation with the Crown Prince of Saudi Arabia, the Prime Minister also reiterated India's condemnation of attacks on regional energy infrastructure

title: Kerala Assembly Elections 2026: Not a good precedent for Governors to contest elections immediately after office, says Sreedharan Pillai
content: The BJP leader, who served as Governor of Mizoram and Goa, talks about his decision not to contest the Assembly polls from Chengannur, the BJP-led NDA’s chances, and the focused attacks on the BJP by the LDF and the UDF

title: What did the Supreme Court rule on Scheduled Castes and religion?
content: What did the Supreme Court say about Scheduled Caste status for those not belonging to Hinduism, Sikhism, or Buddhism? Can SC status be restored through reconversion? What is the Centre’s position on SC status for Dalit Muslims and Christians? What about Scheduled Tribes?

title: Telangana CM Revanth Reddy conveys his resolve to go ahead with Musi development
content: Telangana CM Revanth Reddy said a mosque is scheduled to be constructed at Charminar, besides Gurdwara at Sikh Chawni in Gowliguda and church on the Nagole-Uppal stretch

title: Thunderstorm forecast in 19 districts of Telangana on March 29
content: The maximum and minimum temperatures are likely to be around 38°C and 25°C respectively,” according to IMD bulletin

title: West Asia crisis: Rajnath Singh calls for swift decisions, long-term readiness
content: Mr. Singh made the remarks while chairing the first meeting of the Informal Group of Ministers (IGoM) set up to monitor the situation in West Asia

title: Government’s failure responsible for supply crunch, panic among people, says Pramod Tiwari
content: Mr. Tiwari further alleged that the BJP Government repeatedly makes such statements, which create an atmosphere of panic in the country

title: OBC and EBC voters are not happy with Nitish Kumar moving to Rajya Sabha, says Anand Mohan
content: Expressing his apprehension, Mr. Mohan added that in days to come or in near future, the ruling parties will not get any benefit from the decision of Nitish Kumar leaving Bihar politics and moving to Rajya Sabha

title: Great Indian Bustard chick born in Gujarat after a decade through jumpstart approach: Environment Minister
content: The field monitoring team is observing the young chick being reared by its foster mother in its natural habitat after a significant achievement in the recovery of the critically endangered species

title: Why is there controversy over the Central Armed Police Forces (General Administration) Bill?
content: Why was the Bill introduced in the Rajya Sabha? Why is there opposition to it? What is the government’s justification?

title: Chhattisgarh Govt cracks down on LPG cylinder hoarding, 97 FIRs registered
content: A comprehensive review of the availability and supply of petroleum products, LPG cylinders, fertilisers and other essential commodities was undertaken during a high-level review meeting chaired by Chief Minister Vishnu Deo Sai

title: T.N. Assembly election: Vijay calls on CEO, given nod to hold public meeting at Perambur in Chennai
content: In a petition submitted to the official, he says law and order mechanisms were not being applied uniformly, seeks immediate transfer of key officials including the Director-General of Police

title: Finance Bill fails to address LPG shortage, says DMK MP P. Wilson
content: India is now among the world’s largest LPG consumers, with annual demand touching nearly 31 million tonnes, but has storage capacity sufficient for barely three to four weeks

title: Former AIADMK leaders allotted DMK tickets
content: O. Panneerselvam gets Bodinayakanur; his supporters R. Vaithilingam and Manoj Pandian have been fielded in Orathanadu and Alangulam

title: Tiger poisoned to death near Satpura Reserve; five arrested
content: According to officials, the decomposed body of the female tiger was found buried outside the Reserve’s boundary in the Sangakheda range of the West Chhindwara Division after which the forest officials swung into action.

title: J&K House condemns attack on Abdullah; Minister Itoo questions silence of police, Home dept.
content: J&K Peoples Conference legislator Sajad Lone said the governments in J&K “have been using security cover for political ends”

title: T.N. Assembly election: Premallatha to contest in Vriddhachalam; Virudhunagar allotted to Vijayaprabakaran
content: The party was allotted 10 Assembly constituencies and a Rajya Sabha seat by the DMK. The party’s treasurer L.K. Sudhish was elected as a Rajya Sabha Member recently

title: T.N. Assembly election: DMK candidate list includes 60 fresh faces and 4 Mayors
content: The list also includes 19 women candidates. Of the 164 nominees, 15 are doctors, 17 engineers, 29 advocates, and seven hold Ph.Ds

title: BIMS’ golden jubilee fete ends, alumni association takes shape
content: Past students felicitate D. Anand, senior professor, who is retiring after an impressive journey in the Department of Studies in Business Administration

title: When defeat becomes apparent person loses control over words, says Samajwadi Party on PM Modi’s ‘ATM for loot’ jibe
content: Opposition party states Noida evolved as an economic engine because of planned industrial clusters, affordable housing, metro connectivity, and opportunities for small and medium enterprises initiated by previous SP government

title: Justice Nagarathna calls for making fragile, sensitive ecosystems absolute no-go zones
content: The judiciary is compelled to step in and drive environmental governance due to legislative gaps, regulatory inertia, and rapidly evolving environmental risk, says the Supreme Court judge

title: Bengaluru South City Corporation budget 2026–27 focuses on infra push with ₹3,826-crore outlay
content: A key administrative reform includes the creation of 72 new wards with plans to establish ward offices to bring governance closer to citizens.

title: 2 Indian, 1 Pakistani ships cross Strait of Hormuz
content: Two other LPG carriers belonging to the state-owned SCI had crossed the strait and have discharged their cargo in India.

title: LPG shortage cripples hostels and small hotels in Vizianagaram
content: Over 15,000 bachelors and job aspirants struggle for food as commercial gas scarcity forces closures and black market rates surge to ₹6,000

title: NDA will retain power in Assam with more than 90 seats, says Amit Shah
content: Participating in a roadshow for BJP’s Guwahati Central candidate Vijay Kumar Gupta, the Union Home Minister underlines performance by the incumbent government and the Centre’s infrastructure push

title: Amit Shah releases ‘charge sheet’ against Trinamool government in West Bengal
content: Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee has “always played the politics of the victim card”, says Mr. Shah; BJP trying to blur the lines between Bengalis and Bangladeshis for electoral gain, claims TMC

title: LPG crisis: Cooking gas crunch revives firewood economy in rural and semi-urban Karnataka
content: The surge in demand, triggered by supply constraints linked to the ongoing West Asia crisis, has given rise to a parallel, largely informal firewood market in many districts

title: Economist recommends single, uniform GST rate applied to all domestic consumption
content: Recommends redistributing the entire revenue equally to every citizen as a per capita digital transfer via Aadhaar-linked accounts

title: Gobar gas plants at homes provide a shield to residents of Belagavi’s Kattanbavi amid cylinder shortage
content: Most of the houses in this village of around 1,200 people have gobar gas plants; they were set-up 30-35 years ago, but are still functioning

title: Wagler leads Illini to first Final Four in 21 years
content: Keaton Wagler scored 25 points in Illinois' 71-59 win over Iowa on Saturday, sending the program to the Final Four for the first time in 21 years.

title: USMNT routed by Belgium in blow to WCup prep
content: The USMNT suffered a setback in its preparations for the World Cup as Belgium ran rampant in the second half to emerge with a 5-2 win at Atlanta's Mercedes-Benz Stadium on Saturday.

title: TCU women top UVA to reach 2nd straight Elite 8
content: Marta Suarez formed a nearly unstoppable duo with Olivia Miles on Saturday to lead No. 3 seed TCU past 10th-seeded Virginia and into the Sweet 16.

title: S.C. cruises into Elite Eight with 'room to grow'
content: South Carolina has looked dominant in their first three wins in the women's NCAA tournament, but the Gamecocks maintain they're yet to hit their ceiling.

title: UFC fighter called back to cage after scorecard flub
content: Tyrell Fortune had to return to the Octagon on Saturday after UFC cage announcer Bruce Buffer mistakenly announced his opponent Marcin Tybura as the winner.

title: Mayweather pumps brakes on Pacquiao rematch
content: Floyd Mayweather cast some doubt on details of his pending fight with Manny Pacquiao, saying the location has not be set and that the bout will actually just be an exhibition.

title: Doncic hit with 1-game suspension for 16th tech
content: Lakers star Luka Doncic will be suspended for Monday's game against the Wizards after receiving his 16th technical foul on Friday.

title: Geno takes aim at NCAA tournament setup, prep
content: UConn women's basketball coach Geno Auriemma has questions about the NCAA's decisions on its tournament and seeks more input for coaches and players into tournament matters.

title: 📸 LeBron to Bronny: Father-son assist makes history
content: The father-son duo etched their names in the history books after the elder James found his son for a 3-pointer.

title: Women's March Madness 2026: Ranking the Elite Eight
content: All four No. 1 seeds advanced to the regional final. The ACC, Big Ten and SEC each have two teams in the field.

title: NBA playoff watch: 76ers, Hornets clash in key seeding matchup
content: Can Philadelphia or Charlotte move into the top six? Plus, updated playoff projections, draft lottery standings after Thursday.

title: USMNT handed reality check by Doku, Belgium ahead of World Cup
content: Belgium provided a sobering assessment of where the USMNT currently stands, doling out a heavy 5-2 defeat that will leave Mauricio Pochettino with plenty to ponder.

title: Player ratings: Weah poor as USMNT suffer heavy Belgium defeat
content: It was a difficult game for the U.S. against Belgium, with Tim Weah struggling to make a positive impact.

title: WNBA's CBA: 10 biggest wins from new agreement
content: The CBA permanently and positively changes the WNBA's landscape. These are the wins, biggest strides and areas of compromise.

title: Man City show why they are worthy WSL title winners as tired United wilt
content: Women's Super League champions-elect Manchester City thrashed second-place Man United to go 11 points clear and show why they are the best around.

title: A former NFL GM's must-draft board for 2026: Tannenbaum pounds the table for 14 prospects
content: From Dillon Thieneman on Day 1 to Jaffer Murphy late on Day 3, Mike Tannenbaum picks the draft prospects he'd want to target across all seven rounds.

title: Latest NFL draft buzz: Ty Simpson's pro day and more
content: How high could QB Ty Simpson get picked? Is Jeremiyah Love really a possibility for the Titans at No. 4?

title: Ilia Malinin leaves Milan Cortina Olympics behind with world championship
content: After a disappointing performance in the 2026 Winter Olympics, Ilia Malinin looked every bit the best men's figure skater alive at the world championships in Prague.

title: Lehigh Valley IronPigs poke fun at Zack Wheeler's rib removal in rehab promo
content: The Phillies' ace had a rib removed as part of a surgery to remove a blood clot -- and kept it in his closet.

title: Tyrell Fortune: Living to fight another day after they pronounced me dead
content: Bellator heavyweight Tyrell Fortune recalls the weight cut that nearly took his life and left him with a new outlook.

title: Transfer rumors, news: Salah eyes Roma after Liverpool exit
content: Liverpool winger Mohamed Salah is considering a return to Roma when he moves on in the summer. Transfer Talk has the latest.

title: Draft guide: Rankings, tips, strategy, stats and more
content: All the rankings, projections, cheat sheets, strategy and analysis you need for the fantasy baseball season.

title: KSRTC wins SKOCH National Award 2025 for transparent recruitment programme
content: e-Paper The View From India Looking at World Affairs from the Indian perspective . first day first show news and reviews from the world of cinema and streaming . the weekly newsletter from science writers takes the jargon out of science and puts the fun in!

title: Great Indian Bustard Chick Born In Kutch After 10 Years
content: The number of birds at the breeding centres established at Sam and Ramdevara in Rajasthan has now increased to 73.

title: Iran Threatens To Hit US University Campuses In Middle East
content: Several US universities have campuses scattered throughout the Gulf region, such as Texas A&M University in Qatar and New York University in the United Arab Emirates.

title: Millions Join "No Kings" Protests In US, Europe Over Iran War, Trump Policies
content: People rallied from New York City, with almost 8.5 million residents in a solidly blue state, to Driggs, a town of fewer than 2,000 people in eastern Idaho, a state Trump carried with 66% of the vote in 2024.

title: Over 4 Lakh Kitkat Bars Stolen After Leaving Factory In Italy For Poland
content: The shipment of the crunchy bars, made of waffles covered with chocolate, disappeared last week while en route between production and distribution locations.

title: Speeding Thar Kills 2 Children, Their Grandfather In Gurugram
content: Zayed Khan and Ishant, aged eight and 10, died on the spot, while their maternal grandfather, 60-year-old Subhash, died in a hospital.

title: Iran War LIVE: Israel Army Says Struck Iran Complex Producing Naval Weapons
content: The war in the Middle East began when the US and Israel conducted strikes on Iran, killing Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei and other senior leaders.

title: "Kissing My A**": Trump Takes A Jibe, Then Praises Saudi Crown Prince
content: Speaking at a Saudi-backed investment forum in Florida, Donald Trump said that the Saudi Crown Prince did not expect the US to make a strong comeback under his presidency.

title: Thousands Of Marines Add To Largest US Buildup In Middle East In 20 Years
content: Photos posted by CENTCOM show soldiers in combat fatigues wearing helmets and goggles. The Marines supplement the 50,000 US troops already stationed in the Middle East.

title: On Camera, Odisha MLA Fires Shot In Air During Ram Navami Procession
